title: prunus avium subsp. duracina
intro: Prunus avium subsp. duracina, also known as the wild cherry, is a subspecies of cherry tree that is native to Europe and Western Asia. The bark, leaves, and fruits of this plant have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant properties. The health benefits of Prunus avium subsp. duracina include relief from respiratory issues such as coughs and colds, as well as treatment for digestive problems and skin irritations. The herbal preparations of Prunus avium subsp. duracina include teas, tinctures, and infusions made from the bark and leaves, which can be used to soothe sore throats and reduce fever.
